skill.name: elixir-pro
skill.description: Guides idiomatic Elixir development with OTP, supervision trees, Phoenix LiveView, Ecto, testing, and BEAM performance practices.
skill.summary: Build fault-tolerant Elixir and Phoenix systems with OTP guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found the external command alert at SKILL.md:26 is a false positive from markdown backticks around a file path. The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts are false positives from ordinary Elixir prose, not crypto APIs or algorithms. No prompt injection, exfiltration, network access, filesystem abuse, or executable code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick File Reference
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:26 as shell backtick execution. The line uses markdown backticks around resources/implementation-playbook.md and does not execute a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is an inline markdown file reference, not a Ruby or shell execution expression. No executable syntax or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithms at SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:5, SKILL.md:37, SKILL.md:44, and SKILL.md:59. These lines describe Elixir systems, distributed design, and concurrency concepts; they do not reference cryptographic functions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain no crypto library calls, algorithm names, hashing code, or security-sensitive operations. The matches come from normal technical prose in an Elixir guidance file.

content.value_statement: Elixir teams need reliable patterns for concurrency, supervision, and Phoenix application design. This skill provides focused guidance for OTP, LiveView, Ecto, testing, and BEAM performance work.
actual_capabilities: Explains OTP patterns such as GenServer, Supervisor, and Application design.
Guides Phoenix and LiveView architecture with contexts and clean boundaries.
Helps design Ecto schemas, changesets, and database interaction patterns.
Suggests ExUnit, doctest, property test, and async testing approaches.
Recommends BEAM profiling, benchmarking, telemetry, and observability practices.
Reviews Elixir code for idiomatic pattern matching, immutability, and process isolation.
limitations: Does not include runnable scripts or automation tools.
Does not inspect private application state unless the user provides it.
Cannot replace production load testing or operational monitoring.
References an optional playbook file that is not present in this package.

use_cases.title: Design an OTP Service
use_cases.description: Plan a supervised process structure for a stateful Elixir service with clear restart behavior.

use_cases.title: Improve Phoenix Boundaries
use_cases.description: Refactor Phoenix contexts, LiveView flows, and Ecto usage for clearer ownership and maintainability.

use_cases.title: Review BEAM Performance
use_cases.description: Identify profiling, benchmarking, and telemetry steps for a concurrency or throughput issue.

prompt_templates.title: Review Elixir Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Elixir module for idiomatic pattern matching, clear function design, and simple tests. Explain the most important changes first.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Supervision
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an OTP supervision tree for this service. Include worker responsibilities, restart strategy, state ownership, and failure cases.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Phoenix Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Phoenix or LiveView flow. Focus on contexts, Ecto changesets, validation boundaries, and test coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose BEAM Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Elixir performance problem. Propose profiling steps, telemetry events, benchmark design, and likely OTP bottlenecks.

output_examples.input: Review a GenServer that keeps mutable state and crashes during reconnects.
output: A prioritized review of state ownership and callback behavior.
A supervision recommendation with restart behavior and recovery tradeoffs.
A focused testing plan for reconnect, timeout, and crash scenarios.

output_examples.input: Improve a Phoenix LiveView form backed by Ecto changesets.
output: A boundary review for LiveView, context, schema, and changeset responsibilities.
Suggested validation flow for live feedback and final submission.
Test coverage ideas for invalid input, persistence errors, and UI updates.

output_examples.input: Investigate slow message processing in an Elixir application.
output: A profiling plan using BEAM-focused tools and measurements.
Likely bottleneck categories, including mailbox growth and blocking work.
Telemetry and benchmarking recommendations for repeatable validation.

best_practices: Provide the relevant modules, process responsibilities, and failure modes before asking for OTP design.
Ask for tests and observability changes together when reviewing production behavior.
Validate suggestions against your application supervision tree, deployment topology, and database constraints.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for unrelated languages or general project management tasks.
Do not ask it to replace production incident review or load testing.
Do not accept OTP changes without testing restart behavior and state recovery.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps with Elixir, OTP, Phoenix, LiveView, Ecto, testing, and BEAM performance guidance.

faq.question: Does it run Elixir commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill contains guidance only and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it design supervision trees?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose process roles, restart strategies, and failure handling based on your requirements.

faq.question: Can it review Phoenix LiveView code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review LiveView state, context boundaries, changesets, and test strategy.

faq.question: Can it optimize production performance?
faq.answer: It can suggest profiling, benchmarking, and telemetry steps. You must validate results in your environment.

faq.question: Is the referenced playbook included?
faq.answer: No. The package references resources/implementation-playbook.md, but that file is not included.
